violations.description: General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface * was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. - The stainless steel ice scoop holder has a protective skin applied from the manufacturer that is to be removed before use. This skin was not removed and was chipping and flacking, and sticking to the ice scoop.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors throughout the facility require a more frequent or more thorough cleaning. Especially in and round the CIP (clean in place equipment).

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. - When mops are not in use they should be hung to drip and dry. Mop water should be replaced when not in use or the water becomes dirty. A mop was found with the mop head in the wringer and mop water was murky.
